political.s: FEC: $128K in executive political donations; partisan lean split across 8 executive donors.

charity.s: $70M reported corporate giving in 2024 (~0.08% of revenue) via P&G Fund.

dei.s: Via parent company Procter & Gamble: HRC Corporate Equality Index: 100/100 — Equality 100 Leader (2025). Top-rated LGBTQ+ workplace policies, benefits, and culture. Disability:IN Equality Index score 100/100. JUST Capital JUST 100 — ranked #28 of Russell 1000 (2025) for treatment of workers, customers, communities, environment. Bloomberg Gender-Equality Index member (2024) — transparent on female leadership, equal pay, inclusive culture. Held by 1 ESG/SRI fund(s) (gender-equality-funds) — investor-screen positive signal. HRC Corporate Equality Index 100/100 (2025) — top-tier LGBTQ+ workplace equality rating.

animals.s: Via parent company Procter & Gamble: Wikipedia "Animal testing" section: Procter & Gamble has received criticism from animal advocacy group PETA for the practice of testing on animals. On June 30, 1999, Procter & Gamble announced that it would limit its animal testing…

privacy.s: Via parent company Procter & Gamble: CFPB consumer complaints: 390 on record (33 in past 12mo); 100% timely response rate.

execPay.s: Via parent company Procter & Gamble: CEO-to-median-worker pay ratio 276:1 (median employee pay $79,510) — SEC proxy statement (DEF 14A, 2025).

sampleRecords.event_description: Caught in or compressed by equipment or objects, unspecified
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was reaching down to pick up a cart handle when the tip of his right index finger was caught between a trailer and its liftgate, resulting in a fractured fingertip.

sampleRecords.part_of_body: Abdomen, except internal location of diseases or disorders
sampleRecords.nature: Internal injuries to organs and blood vessels of the trunk
sampleRecords.event_description: Other fall to lower level, unspecified
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee slipped on a truck's lift gate and fell, suffering broken ribs and a ruptured spleen. The employee was hospitalized.

sampleRecords.event_description: Compressed or pinched by shifting objects or equipment
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was pushing a dryer rack cart when his left hand was caught between two carts, amputating the tip of the third digit on his left hand.

sampleRecords.event_description: Caught in running equipment or machinery during maintenance, cleaning
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was repairing equipment. The equipment was not locked out. The reciprocator arm contacted the employee's right arm resulting in a laceration that severed an artery requiring surgery.

sampleRecords.event_description: Other fall to lower level less than 6 feet
sampleRecords.final_narrative: On or about July 29, a temporary employee assigned to demo a commercial building was standing on a six foot ladder pulling speaker and telephone wire when he fell 4-6 feet to the concrete floor breaking his arm and cutting his shoulder.
